# Authentic hadiths (Sahih al-Bukhari & Sahih Muslim) via fawazahmed0/hadith-api.
# Auto-generated by refresh-hadiths.sh — edits here are overwritten on refresh.
Narrated Abu Huraira: "Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "By Him in Whose Hands my life is, none of you will have faith till he loves me more than his father and his children  — Bukhari 14
Narrated Anas: The Prophet (saw) said "None of you will have faith till he loves me more than his father, his children and all mankind  — Bukhari 15
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:Once the Prophet (saw) embraced me and said, "O Allah! Bestow on him the knowledge of the Book (Qur'an)  — Bukhari 75
Narrated Anas bin Malik:One night an orphan and I offered the prayers behind the Prophet (saw) in my house and my mother (Um Sulaim) was standing behind us (by herself forming a row)  — Bukhari 727
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "If any one of you says, "Amin" and the angels in the heavens say "Amin" and the former coincides with the latter, all his past sins will be forgiven  — Bukhari 781
Narrated Anas:The qunut [supplication before going down for prostration] used to be recited in the Maghrib and the Fajr prayers  — Bukhari 798
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (saw) prayed in the house of Um Sulaim; and I, along with an orphan stood behind him while Um Sulaim (stood) behind us  — Bukhari 871
Narrated Abu Hummaid As-Sa`idi:One night Allah's Messenger (saw) stood up after the prayer and recited "Tashah-hud" and then praised Allah as He deserved and said, "Amma ba'du  — Bukhari 925
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (saw) said, "A Muslim whose three children die before the age of puberty will be granted Paradise by Allah due to his mercy for them  — Bukhari 1248
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (saw) said, "The real patience is at the first stroke of a calamity  — Bukhari 1302
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "Any Muslim whose three children died before the age of puberty will be granted Paradise by Allah because of His mercy to them  — Bukhari 1381
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said, "The best charity is that which is practiced by a wealthy person. And start giving first to your dependents  — Bukhari 1426
Narrated `Ali:Allah's Messenger (saw) ordered me to give in charity the skin and the coverings of the Budn which I had slaughtered  — Bukhari 1707
Narrated As-Sa'ib bin Yazid:(While in the company of my parents) I was made to perform Hajj with Allah's Messenger (saw) and I was a seven-year-old boy then. (Fath-ul-Bari, p.443, Vol)  — Bukhari 1858
Narrated Hisham's father:Aisha said, "Allah's Messenger (saw) used to kiss some of his wives while he was fasting," and then she smiled  — Bukhari 1928
Narrated Aisha:Allah's Messenger (saw) forbade Al-Wisal out of mercy to them. They said to him, "But you practice Al- Wisal?" He said, "I am not similar to you, for my Lord gives me food and drink  — Bukhari 1964
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said, "If a woman gives something (i.e. in charity) from her husband's earnings without his permission, she will get half his reward  — Bukhari 2066
Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "May Allah's mercy be on him who is lenient in his buying, selling, and in demanding back his money  — Bukhari 2076
Narrated Aisha:I said, "O Allah's Messenger (saw)! I have two neighbors and would like to know to which of them I should give presents." He replied, "To the one whose door is nearer to you  — Bukhari 2259
Narrated Abu Musa Al-Ash`ari:The Prophet (saw) said, "The honest treasurer who gives willingly what he is ordered to give, is one of the two charitable persons, (the second being the owner)  — Bukhari 2260
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (saw) passed a date fallen on the way and said, "Were I not afraid that it may be from a Sadaqa (charitable gifts), I would have eaten it  — Bukhari 2431
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "Help your brother, whether he is an oppressor or he is an oppressed one  — Bukhari 2443
Narrated Ibn `Umar:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "If a slave is honest and faithful to his master and worships his Lord (Allah) in a perfect manner, he will get a double reward  — Bukhari 2546
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said, "Goodness and comfort are for him who worships his Lord in a perfect manner and serves his master sincerely  — Bukhari 2549
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (saw) said, "If a slave serves his Saiyid (i.e. master) sincerely and worships his Lord (Allah) perfectly, he will get a double reward  — Bukhari 2550
Narrated Abu Musa:The Prophet (saw) said, "The Mamluk (slave) who worships his Lord in a perfect manner, and is dutiful, sincere and obedient to his Saiyid (master), will get a double reward  — Bukhari 2551
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said, "O Muslim women! None of you should look down upon the gift sent by her female neighbor even if it were the trotters of the sheep (fleshless part of legs)  — Bukhari 2566
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Some meat was brought to the Prophet (saw) and it was said that the meat had been given in charity to Barirah. He said, "It was Sadaqa for Barirah but a gift for us  — Bukhari 2577
Narrated Aisha:I said, "O Allah's Messenger (saw)! I have two neighbors; which of them should I give a gift to?" The Prophet (saw) said, "(Give) to the one whose door is nearer to you  — Bukhari 2595
Narrated `Amr bin Al-Harith:The Prophet (saw) did not leave anything behind him after his death except a white mule, his arms and a piece of land which he left to be given in charity  — Bukhari 2873
Narrated `Amr bin Al-Harith:The Prophet (saw) did not leave behind him after his death, anything except his arms, his white mule, and a piece of land at Khaibar which he left to be given in charity  — Bukhari 2912
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "When Allah completed the creation, He wrote in His Book which is with Him on His Throne, "My Mercy overpowers My Anger  — Bukhari 3194
Narrated Abu Talha:I heard Allah's Messenger (saw) saying; "Angels (of Mercy) do not enter a house wherein there is a dog or a picture of a living creature (a human being or an animal)  — Bukhari 3225
Narrated Salim's father:Once Gabriel promised the Prophet (that he would visit him, but Gabriel did not come) and later on he said, "We, angels, do not enter a house which contains a picture or a dog  — Bukhari 3227
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said, "Allah has made an opening in the wall of the Gog and Magog (people) like this, and he made with his hand (with the help of his fingers)  — Bukhari 3347
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said, "May Allah forgive Lot: He wanted to have a powerful support  — Bukhari 3375
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said, "May Allah save the tribe of Aslam, and may Allah forgive the tribe of Ghifar  — Bukhari 3514
Um Salama said:The Prophet (saw) woke up and said, "Glorified be Allah: What great (how many) treasures have been sent down, and what great (how many ) afflictions have been sent down  — Bukhari 3599
Narrated Usama bin Zaid: That the Prophet (saw) used to take him (i.e. Usama) and Al-Hasan (in his lap) and say: "O Allah! Love them, as I love them  — Bukhari 3735
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, " Every nation has an extremely trustworthy man, and the trustworthy man of this (i.e. Muslim) nation is Abu 'Ubaida bin Al-Jarrah  — Bukhari 3744
Narrated Usama bin Zaid:That the Prophet (saw) used to take him and Al-Hasan, and used to say, "O Allah! I love them, so please love them," or said something similar  — Bukhari 3747
Narrated Al-Bara:I saw the Prophet (saw) carrying Al-Hasan on his shoulder an saying, "O Allah! I love him, so please love him  — Bukhari 3749
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:Once the Prophet (saw) embraced me (pressed me to his chest) and said, "O Allah, teach him wisdom (i.e. the understanding of the knowledge of Qur'an)  — Bukhari 3756
Narrated 'Abdul Warith: The same but said, "O Allah, teach him (Ibn Abbas) the Book (i.e. the understanding of the knowledge of Qur'an)." Narrated Khalid: As above  — Bukhari 3756.2
Narrated Jarir bin 'Abdullah: Allah's Messenger (saw) has never refused to admit me since I embraced Islam, and whenever he saw me, he would smile  — Bukhari 3822
Narrated Abu Masud Al-Badri:The Prophet (saw) said, "A man's spending on his family is a deed of charity  — Bukhari 4006
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (saw) said, "This is a mountain that loves us and is loved by us  — Bukhari 4083
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (saw) said, "Every nation has an Amin (i.e. the most honest man), and the Amin of this nation is Abu 'Ubaida bin Al-Jarrah  — Bukhari 4382
Narrated Ikrima:Ibn `Abbas said ( regarding the verse), "And when the relatives and the orphans and the poor are present at the time of division, "this verse and its order is valid and not abrogated  — Bukhari 4576
Abdullah bin Az-Zubair said:Allah ordered His Prophet to forgive the people their misbehavior (towards him)  — Bukhari 4644
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:The Prophet (saw) said, "The keys of the Unseen are five." And then he recited: 'Verily, the knowledge of the Hour is with Allah (alone)  — Bukhari 4778
Narrated Al-Bara':I learnt, 'Glorify the Name of your Lord the Most High' (Surat al-A'la) No 87, before the Prophet (saw) came (to Medina)  — Bukhari 4995
Narrated Aisha:The Prophet (saw) heard a man reciting the Qur'an in the mosque and said, "May Allah bestow His Mercy on him, as he has reminded me of such-and-such Verses of such a Surah  — Bukhari 5037
Narrated Sahl:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "I and the one who looks after an orphan will be like this in Paradise," showing his middle and index fingers and separating them  — Bukhari 5304
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "The best alms is that which you give when you are rich, and you should start first to support your dependants  — Bukhari 5356
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said, "If the wife gives of her husband's property (something in charity) without his permission, he will get half the reward  — Bukhari 5360
Narrated Anas:To the best of my knowledge, the Prophet (saw) did not take his meals in a big tray at all, nor did he ever eat well-baked thin bread, nor did he ever eat at a dining table  — Bukhari 5386
Narrated `Aisha:Allah's Messenger (saw) used to love sweet edible things and honey  — Bukhari 5431
Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (saw) said, "Whoever keeps a (pet) dog which is neither a watch dog nor a hunting dog, will get a daily deduction of two Qirat from his good deeds  — Bukhari 5480
Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "If someone keeps a dog neither for guarding livestock, nor for hunting, his good deeds will decrease (in reward) by two Qirats a day  — Bukhari 5482
Narrated `Aisha:I heard the Prophet (saw) , who was resting against me, saying, "O Allah! Excuse me and bestow Your Mercy on me and let me join with the highest companions (in Paradise)." See Qur'an  — Bukhari 5674
Narrated Hisham's father:`Aisha used to recommend at-Talbina and used to say, "It is disliked (by the patient) although it is beneficial  — Bukhari 5690
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (saw) used to say to the patient, "In the Name of Allah The earth of our land and the saliva of some of us cure our patient  — Bukhari 5745
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (saw) said, "No 'Adwa (no contagious disease is conveyed to others without Allah's permission), nor Tiyara, but I like the good Fal, i.e., the good word  — Bukhari 5756
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (saw) said, "No 'Adwa nor Tiyara; but I like Fal." They said, "What is the Fal?" He said, "A good word  — Bukhari 5776
Narrated Al-Bara:The Prophet (saw) was of a modest height. I saw him wearing a red suit, and I did not see anything better than him  — Bukhari 5848
Narrated Jubair bin Mut`im:That he heard the Prophet (saw) saying, "The person who severs the bond of kinship will not enter Paradise  — Bukhari 5984
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Apostle said, "Whoever loves that he be granted more wealth and that his lease of life be prolonged then he should keep good relations with his Kith and kin  — Bukhari 5986
Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:The Prophet (saw) said, "I and the person who looks after an orphan and provides for him, will be in Paradise like this," putting his index and middle fingers together  — Bukhari 6005
Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (saw) said, "If any Muslim plants any plant and a human being or an animal eats of it, he will be rewarded as if he had given that much in charity  — Bukhari 6012
Narrated Jarir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (saw) said, "He who is not merciful to others, will not be treated mercifully  — Bukhari 6013
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (saw) said "Gabriel continued to recommend me about treating the neighbors Kindly and politely so much so that I thought he would order me to make them as my heirs  — Bukhari 6014
Narrated `Aisha:I said, "O Allah's Messenger (saw)! I have two neighbors! To whom shall I send my gifts?" He said, "To the one whose gate in nearer to you  — Bukhari 6020
Narrated `Aisha:I never saw the Prophet (saw) laughing to an extent that one could see his palate, but he always used to smile only  — Bukhari 6092
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "The strong is not the one who overcomes the people by his strength, but the strong is the one who controls himself while in anger  — Bukhari 6114
Narrated Ubai bin Ka`b:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "Some poetry contains wisdom  — Bukhari 6145
Narrated `Abdullah:The Prophet (saw) said, "Everyone will be with those whom he loves  — Bukhari 6168
Narrated Abu Musa:It was said to the Prophet; , "A man may love some people but he cannot catch up with their good deeds?" The Prophet (saw) said, "Everyone will be with those whom he loves  — Bukhari 6170
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "They say Al-Karm (the generous), and in fact Al-Karm is the heart of a believer  — Bukhari 6183
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said (in the above narration No. 268), "And then raise your head till you feel at ease while sitting  — Bukhari 6252
Narrated `Aisha:That the Prophet (saw) said to her, "Gabriel sends Salam (greetings) to you." She replied, "Wa 'alaihi-s- Salam Wa Rahmatu-l-lah." (Peace and Allah's Mercy be on him)  — Bukhari 6253
Narrated Abu Huraira:I heard Allah's Messenger (saw) saying." By Allah! I ask for forgiveness from Allah and turn to Him in repentance more than seventy times a day  — Bukhari 6307
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "Allah is more pleased with the repentance of His slave than anyone of you is pleased with finding his camel which he had lost in the desert  — Bukhari 6309
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "Whoever says, 'Subhan Allah wa bihamdihi,' one hundred times a day, will be forgiven all his sins even if they were as much as the foam of the sea  — Bukhari 6405
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (saw) said, "O Allah! There is no life worth living except the life of the Hereafter, so (please) make righteous the Ansar and the Emigrants  — Bukhari 6413
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "The son of Adam (i.e. man) grows old and so also two (desires) grow old with him, i.e., love for wealth and (a wish for) a long life  — Bukhari 6421
Narrated Abu Musa:The Prophet (saw) said: "Whoever loves to meet Allah, Allah loves to meet him; and whoever hates to meet Allah, Allah hates to meet him  — Bukhari 6508
Narrated Abu Qatada:The Prophet (saw) said, "Relieved or relieving. And a believer is relieved (by death)  — Bukhari 6513
Narrated Al-Bara:The Prophet (saw) ordered us to help others to fulfill the oaths  — Bukhari 6654
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said, "Allah forgives my followers those (evil deeds) their souls may whisper or suggest to them as long as they do not act (on it) or speak  — Bukhari 6664
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (saw) said, "Our (Apostles') property should not be inherited, and whatever we leave, is to be spent in charity  — Bukhari 6727
Narrated `Aisha:The Prophet (saw) cut off the hand of a lady, and that lady used to come to me, and I used to convey her message to the Prophet (saw) and she repented, and her repentance was sincere  — Bukhari 6800
Narrated Anas bin Malik:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "A good dream (that comes true) of a righteous man is one of forty-six parts of prophetism  — Bukhari 6983
Narrated Abu Huraira:I heard the truthful and trusted by Allah (i.e., the Prophet (saw) ) saying, "The destruction of my followers will be through the hands of young men from Quraish  — Bukhari 7058
Narrated Anas:The Prophet (saw) said, "For every nation there is an Amin (honest, trustworthy person) and the Amin of this nation is Abu 'Ubaida  — Bukhari 7255
Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (saw) embraced me and said, "O Allah! Teach him (the knowledge of) the Book (Qur'an)  — Bukhari 7270
Narrated Jarir bin `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "Allah will not be merciful to those who are not merciful to mankind  — Bukhari 7376
Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (saw) said, "When Allah had finished His creation, He wrote over his Throne: 'My Mercy preceded My Anger  — Bukhari 7422
Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (saw) said, "When Allah created the creations, He wrote with Him on His Throne: 'My Mercy has preceded My Anger  — Bukhari 7453
It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira that the Prophet (saw) said:Iman has over seventy branches, and modesty is a branch of Iman  — Muslim 152
Zuhri has narrated this hadith with the addition of these words:He (the Holy Prophet) happened to pass by a mass of Ansar who was instructing his brother (about modesty)  — Muslim 155
It is narrated on the authority of Anas that the Apostle (may peace and blessings be upon him) said:The love of the Ansar is the sign of faith and hatred against them is the sign of dissemblance  — Muslim 236
It is reported on the authority of 'Abdullah that the Messenger of Allah observed:The best of' the deeds or deed is the (observance of) prayer at its proper time and kindness to the parents  — Muslim 256
Abdullah b. Umar said:I went up to the roof of the house of my sister Hafsa and saw the Messenger of Allah (saw) relieving himself facing Syria. with his back to the Qibla  — Muslim 612
Aba Qatada reported:The Messenger of Allah (way peace be upon him) forbade (us) to breathe into the venel, to touch the penis with the right hand and to wipe after relieving with right hand  — Muslim 615
A'isha reported:The Messenger of Allah (way peace be upon him) loved to start from the right-hand side for performing ablution, for combing (the hair) and wearing the shoes  — Muslim 616
A'isha reported:The Messenger of Allah (saw) loved to start from the right-hand side in his every act i. e. in wearing shoes, in combing (his hair) and in performing ablution  — Muslim 617
Ibn 'Abbas reported:The Apostle (saw) woke up at night; relieved himself, and then washed his face and hands and then again slept  — Muslim 698
A'isha said:The Apostle of Allah (saw) used to remember Allah at all moments  — Muslim 826
Abu Huraira reported:The Messenger of Allah (saw) said: SayAmin when the Imam says Amin, for it anyone's utterance of Amin synchronises with that of the angels, he will be forgiven his past sins  — Muslim 915
Abu Huraira reported:People should avoid lifting their eyes towards the sky while supplicating in prayer, otherwise their eyes would be snatched away  — Muslim 967
A'isha reported that so far as these words of (Allah) Glorious and High are concerned:" And utter not thy prayer loudly, not be low in it" (xvii. 110) relate to supplication (du'a)  — Muslim 1002
Jabir b. Samura reported:The Apostle of Allah (saw) used to recite in the noon prayer:" Glorify the name of thy Most High Lord in the morning prayer longer than this" (lxxxvii)  — Muslim 1030
Ibn Abbas reported from the Messenger of Allah (saw) the words:" And that would fill that which will please Thee besides (them)!" and he did not mention the subsequent (portion of supplication)  — Muslim 1073
Ali reported:My loved one (the Holy Prophet) forbade me that I should recite (the Qur'an) in a state of bowing and prostration  — Muslim 1079
Abu Huraira reported:The Messenger of Allah (saw) said: The nearest a servant comes to his Lord is when he is prostrating himself, so make supplication (in this state)  — Muslim 1083
Abu Huraira reported:The Messenger of Allah (saw) used to say while prostrating himself: O Lord, forgive me all my sins, small and great, first and last, open and secret  — Muslim 1084
Abdullah narrated it on the authority of his father Yahya:Knowledge cannot be acquired with sloth  — Muslim 1390
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying:He who observed prayer at night during Ramadan, because of faith and seeking his reward from Allah, his previous sins would be forgiven  — Muslim 1779
Abu Musa reported Allah's Apostle (saw) as saying:The house in which remembrance of Allah is made and the house in which Allah is not remembered are like the living and the dead  — Muslim 1823
Anas reported:I saw the Messenger of Allah (saw) raising his hands (high enough) in supplication (for rain) that the whiteness of his armpits became visible  — Muslim 2074
Abu Huraira reported from the Messenger of Allah (saw) as having said this:The heart of an old person feels young for the love of two things: love for long life and wealth  — Muslim 2410
Abu Huraira reported that the Messenger of Allah (saw) had said this:The heart of an old person is young for two things: for long life and love for wealth  — Muslim 2411
A'isha (Allah be pleased with her) said that the Messenger of Allah (saw) kissed one of his wives while he was fasting, and then she ('A'isha) smiled (as she narrated)  — Muslim 2573
Anas b. Malik (Allah be pleased with him) reported Allah's Messenger (way peace be upon him) as saying:Uhud is a mountain which loves us and which we love  — Muslim 3372
Abu Huraira (Allah be pleased with him) reported Allah's Messenger (may peace he upon him) as saying:Do not meet the merchandise (in the way)  — Muslim 3822
Anas reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying Never does a Muslim plant trees or cultivate land and birds or a man or a beast eat out of them but that is a charity on his behalf  — Muslim 3973
Ibn 'Abbas (Allah be pleased with them) reported Allah's Apostle (saw) having said this:He who gets back his charity is like a dog which vomits, and then returns to that and eats it  — Muslim 4170
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying:An oath is to be interpreted according to the intention of the one who takes it  — Muslim 4284
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Ghazwan (and the words are):" I heard Abu'l-Qasim (saw) as the Prophet of repentance  — Muslim 4312
It his been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira that the Messenger of Allah (saw) said:" We do not have any heirs; what we leave behind is a charitable endowment  — Muslim 4585
It has been narrated on the authority of Anas b. Malik that the Messenger of Allah (saw) said:O God, there is no life, but the life of the Hereafter, So forgive Thou the Ansar and the Muhajirs  — Muslim 4673
It has been reported on the authority of 'Amr b. al-'As that the Messenger of Allah (saw) said:All the sins of a Shahid (martyr) are forgiven except debt  — Muslim 4883
It has been narrated on the authority of Anas b. Malik that the Messenger of Allah (may peace he upon him) said:Who seeks martyrdom with sincerity shall get its reward, though he may not achieve it  — Muslim 4929
Adi b. Hatim reported:I asked Allah's Messenger (saw) about Mi'rad (i. e. hunting with the help of arrow having a stub end, and he stated the same (as we find in the previous hadith)  — Muslim 4975
(Qatada said:We asked Anas b. Malik which garment did Allah's Messenger may peace be upon him) love or like (to wear). He said: The mantle of Yemen  — Muslim 5440
Abu Sa'id Khudri reported that Allah's Messenger (saw) was more modest than the virgin behind the curtain (or in the apartment), and when he disliked anything, we recognised that from his face  — Muslim 6032
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as having said that Ibrahim (as) circumcised himself with the help of an adze when he was eighty years old  — Muslim 6141
Sa'd b Abi Waqqqs said:Allah's Messenger (may peace he upon him) gathered his parents for me on the Day of Uhud  — Muslim 6235
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying to Hasan:O Allah, behold, I love him. Thou too love him and love one who loves him  — Muslim 6256
Al-Bara' b. Azib reported:I saw Hasan b. 'Ali upon the shoulders of Allah's Apostle (saw) and he was saying: O Allah, I love him, and love him Thou  — Muslim 6258
Al-Bara' b. Azib reported:I saw the Messenger of Allah (saw) with Al-Hasan b. 'Ali placed upon his shoulders and he was saying: O Allah, I love him, and love him Thou  — Muslim 6259
A'isha reported that Allah's Messenger (saw) said to her:Gabriel offered you greetings and I said: So there should be peace and mercy of Allah upon him  — Muslim 6301
Jabir reported that the Throne of the most Compassionate shook because of the death of Sa'd b. Mu'adh  — Muslim 6346
Anas b. Malik reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as say- ing:That his bier (that of Sa'd) was placed (before them) and the Throne of the most Compassionate shook  — Muslim 6347
Jarir b. 'Abdullah said:Allah's Messenger (saw) never refused me permission to see him since I embraced Islam and never looked at me but with a smile  — Muslim 6363
Abu Dharr reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying:To the tribe of Aslam Allah has granted safety and to the tribe of Ghifar Allah has granted pardon  — Muslim 6430
Abdullah b. Umar reported Allah's Apostle (saw) as saying:The finest act of goodness is that a person should treat kindly the loved ones of his father  — Muslim 6514
A'isha reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying:The tie of kinship is suspended to the Throne and says: He who unites me Allah would unite him and he who severed me Allah would sever him  — Muslim 6519
Jubair b. Mutlim reported that his father narrated to him that Allah's Messenger (saw) said:The severer of the tie of kinship would not get into Paradise  — Muslim 6521
Anas b. Malik reported that Allah's Messenger (saw) said:He who likes that his sustenance should be expanded and his age may be lengthened should join the tie of kinship  — Muslim 6524
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying:The strong-man is not one who wrestles well but the strong man is one who controls himself when he is in a fit of rage  — Muslim 6643
A'isha reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying:Gabriel impressed upon me (kind treatment) towards the neighbour (so much) that I thought as if he would confer upon him the (right) of inheritance  — Muslim 6685
Abu Dharr reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying:Abu Dharr, when you prepare the broth, add water to that and give that (as a present) to your neighbour  — Muslim 6688
Abu Dharr reported:Allah's Apostle (saw) said to me: Don't consider anything insignificant out of good things even if it is that you meet your brother with a cheerful countenance  — Muslim 6690
Jarir reported that Allah's Messenger (saw) delivered an address in which he exhorted people to give charity  — Muslim 6801
Ubida b. Samit reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying:He who loves to meet Allah, Allah also loves to meet him, and he who dislikes to meet Allah, Allah also abhors to meet him  — Muslim 6820
Abu Musa reported Allah's Apostle (saw) as saying:He who loves to meet Allah, Allah too loves to meet him, and he who dislikes to meet Allah, Allah abhors to meet him  — Muslim 6828
Anas reported from Allah's Messenger (saw) (this suppli- cation) but with this variation that these words are not found in that supplication:" From the trial of life and death  — Muslim 6874
Anas reported that Allah's Messenger (may peace he upon him) wed to make this supplication:" O Allah, I seek refuge in Thee from miserliness, from sloth and from decrepitude  — Muslim 6876
Abdullah reported that Allah's Messenger (saw) used to supplicate (in these words):" O Allah. I beg of Thee the right guidance, safeguard against evils, chastity and freedom from want  — Muslim 6904
Abu Dharr reported that Allah's Messenger (saw) said:There is no believing servant who supplicates for his brother behind his back (in his absence) that the Angels do not say: The same be for you too  — Muslim 6927
Abu Huraira reported that Allah's Messenger (saw) said:The supplication of every one of you is granted if he does not grow impatient and says: I supplicated but it was not granted  — Muslim 6934
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying:The supplication of one of you is granted if he does not grow impatient and say- I supplicated my Lord but it was not granted  — Muslim 6935
Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger (saw) as saying:Allah is more pleased with the repentance of His servant when he turns penitently towards Him than one of you would be on finding the lost camel  — Muslim 6953
